accept: A single canonical bootstrap command is documented in README and act help, with rationale for the choice over alternatives.
The chosen path is end-to-end tested in a fresh repo, with the resulting agent session validated to use act correctly.
Other distribution options are documented as alternates with their tradeoffs, not as equally-promoted paths.
If the chosen path needs new infrastructure (a tap, an install script, a published release), the dependent issue is implemented or has a clear path forward.
payload.description:
Today getting act into a new project requires choosing among: 'go build -o bin/act ./cmd/act' (only works inside the act repo), 'go install github.com/aac/act/cmd/act@latest' (requires public Go module path), 'gh release download v0.1.0 --pattern act-...' (release is currently draft), or 'brew install ...' (no tap exists yet). Each is filed separately (act-4fe6, act-8416, act-e6a5).

Andrew 2026-05-10: 'do we want something like an act init command instead of dropping .act into a folder which seems a little arcane?' — important clarification: 'act init' already exists; the arcane part is the binary bootstrap, not initialization. Once a binary is on PATH, 'act init' creates .act/ and the skill auto-activates from there.

This issue is the meta-decision: pick ONE canonical 'how a new project starts using act' instruction, as a single shell command (or two: install + init). All other distribution work serves that command. Candidates:

  curl -fsSL https://act.example/install.sh | sh && cd <project> && act init
  brew install aac/act/act && cd <project> && act init
  go install github.com/aac/act/cmd/act@latest && cd <project> && act init

Pick the simplest reliable path; the rest can exist as alternates but are not the canonical pitch. The chosen path informs which of act-4fe6 / act-e6a5 / etc. graduates from 'one option among several' to 'the recommended way'.
payload.title: decide canonical bootstrap: one command to get act working in a new project
